name: Meera Sharma
tagline: Wants the premium version of everything but needs the story to justify it

demographics.education: BBA from a local Jaipur college
demographics.occupation: Junior marketing executive at a textile export firm
demographics.family_status: Single, lives with parents and younger sister

aspiration_drivers: looking like she belongs to a higher income bracket
keeping up with Mumbai/Delhi lifestyle trends
being seen as 'someone who knows good things'
trust_signals: influencer endorsements from people she follows
friends who have already bought it
rejection_triggers: anything her mother would use (seen as outdated)

backstory: Meera grew up in a middle-class Jaipur family where spending on branded items was a luxury reserved for festivals. When she started earning, she dedicated a chunk of her salary to looking and feeling premium. She curates her Instagram carefully, knows all the trending brands from Reels, and saves for pieces that photograph well. She is acutely aware of the gap between her income and her desired lifestyle, and bridges it through careful selection of affordable-premium products. She believes good taste is not about money — it is about knowing what to pick.
current_life_context: She just got her first credit card and is excited but also a little nervous about overspending. Her best friend from college recently posted an unboxing of a new skincare brand from Mumbai and it looked amazing. Meera is now curious about it.
inner_monologue_style: Emotionally driven with a practical undertow. Thinks: 'Oh my god this packaging is gorgeous — wait, how much is it? Ok ₹599 I can do that. But will it actually work? Shreya posted about it so it must be decent. Let me save it and check on payday.' Mixes Hindi and English naturally.
